title: Anaergia, Eni, and CREvolution Launch Circular Project to Support HVO and SAF Production at Gela
content: Anaergia, Eni, and CREvolution have launched a breakthrough circular economy project at Eniâ€™s Gela biorefinery in Italy. As part of a â‚¬50 million initiative, Anaergia secured a C$13 million contract to supply proprietary anaerobic digestion technology. The system is designed to process "degumming soil," a challenging waste byproduct of Hydrotreated Vegetable Oil (HVO) and Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) production, which is typically sent to landfills. By integrating thermal treatment with digestion, the facility aims to recover over 80% of this material and generate more than 70,000 MWh of Renewable Natural Gas (RNG) annually. Scheduled for commissioning by May 2027, the project reduces operational costs and carbon intensity, establishing a replicable model for the rapidly expanding biofuels sector.
author: Analysts Account
